title: Anesthesiologist Assistants
description: Assist anesthesiologists in the administration of anesthesia for surgical and non-surgical procedures. Monitor patient status and provide patient care during surgical treatment.

tasks.text: Provide airway management interventions including tracheal intubation, fiber optics, or ventilary support.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Respond to emergency situations by providing cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), basic cardiac life support (BLS), advanced cardiac life support (ACLS), or pediatric advanced life support (PALS).
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify availability of operating room supplies, medications, and gases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Pretest and calibrate anesthesia delivery systems and monitors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Participate in seminars, workshops, or other professional activities to keep abreast of developments in anesthesiology.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assist anesthesiologists in monitoring of patients, including electrocardiogram (EKG), direct arterial pressure, central venous pressure, arterial blood gas, hematocrit, or routine measurement of temperature, respiration, blood pressure or heart rate.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer blood, blood products, or supportive fluids.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Collect and document patients' pre-anesthetic health histories.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist in the provision of advanced life support techniques including those procedures using high frequency ventilation or intra-arterial cardiovascular assistance devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor and document patients' progress during post-anesthesia period.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Administer anesthetic, adjuvant, or accessory drugs under the direction of an anesthesiologist.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist anesthesiologists in performing anesthetic procedures, such as epidural or spinal injections.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide clinical instruction, supervision or training to staff in areas such as anesthesia practices.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assist in the application of monitoring techniques, such as pulmonary artery catheterization, electroencephalographic spectral analysis, echocardiography, or evoked potentials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Collect samples or specimens for diagnostic testing.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
